summary: Session 3 (final - docs sweep, UAT, release 0.32.0): D3 and D4 executed per the S1 locks; the set's end-of-set deliverables are complete. D3: docs/adoption-bootstrap.md rewritten to a URL-stable deprecation/redirect stub (absolute URLs; VS Code users -> the Getting Started form, manual/non-VS-Code users -> quick-start; the path stays alive because <=0.31.0 clients fetch the raw URL at click time); NEW docs/budget-yaml-schema.md created as the canonical budget.yaml contract home documenting the post-migration shape (S1 sec-2.4) with a legacy-compatibility table whose every rule carries provenance (threshold_scope->scope via the migrator _SCOPE_MAP, monthly->per-project+period, absent verification_method->api, absent scope->per-project explicitly marked DERIVED); swept every S1 sec-1.2 reference - ai-led-session-workflow.md (5 spots incl. the compat-rule retarget), quick-start.md (5 refs + a new Without-VS-Code manual-setup note), root README (section rewritten around the form + retirement note), extension README (conversational-setup paragraph dropped, budget step added to form step 1), repository-reference.md (commands row, adoption-bootstrap row -> retired stub, new budget-yaml-schema row), tier-model.md (3 refs), plus two comment tense fixes (budgetYaml.ts / budgetYaml.test.ts now cite budget-yaml-schema.md). D4: Marketplace-only bump 0.31.0->0.32.0 (package.json + package-lock both nodes + CHANGELOG 0.32.0 entry); NO PyPI bump (re-confirmed: no Python runtime reader of budget.yaml); repository-reference release-status + version walk in PRE-PUSH wording - publish PENDING the operator's vsix-v0.32.0 tag push through the require-green-test gate, run id to be recorded post-publish. UAT (the pre-publish gate): per-set ad-hoc checklist authored with 9 operator-walk rows + 5 AI-pre-verified rows (each naming its ProgrammaticVerification); operator walked it on a local candidate .vsix build and PASSED all 14 rows; the editor's unused blank IsOtherItem placeholder rows were removed from the saved record. UNPLANNED operator-reported item resolved in-session: a consumer repo saw close_session leave verificationVerdict null despite a VERIFIED disposition - root cause was consumer install lag (installed router 0.10.0 predates the 0.15.0 resolve_close_verdict feature; canonical 0.18.0 re-confirmed sound, incl. this set's own S1/S2 persisted verdicts; the consumer upgraded to 0.18.0 same day) - authored docs/cross-repo-router-version-lag-notice.md (remediation, what-a-clean-upgrade-looks-like, hand-backfill option for historical nulls, check-pip-show-before-filing habit); no code change. Suites at the S2 baseline on the release candidate: Python 1222 passed/1 skipped; TS mocha 908 passing + the 2 tracked pre-existing Set-026 failures; Playwright Layer 3 LOCAL 18 passed (4.7m); drift guard OK. Verification: R1 gpt-5-4 ISSUES_FOUND (Major Correctness: schema-doc example comment carried the retired doc's claim that ai_router does spend reporting from budget.yaml; Minor Correctness: notice step-reference off by one after a mid-session section insert; Major Completeness: the three new files were untracked so the R1 diffstat evidence omitted them) -> all three fixed, s3-issues.json persisted with resolutions -> R2 gpt-5-4 narrow re-verify VERIFIED. Routed cost this session $0.2601 (set cumulative ~$1.0588). FOLLOW-UP SET CANDIDATES recorded: (1) runtime router-version-lag advisory (>= pins never auto-upgrade; needs offline/fail-open design); (2) budget-scope selector in the Getting Started form (operator UAT walk-2 suggestion; budget.yaml scope field already supports per-session-set/per-session, form hard-writes per-project per the D1 lock).
